title: Broken Stack Overflow Handling: Misbalanced Element Counts

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers representing stack operations, your task is to implement a stack that handles overflow and underflow correctly. The array contains push, pop, max, and min operations. You need to count the number of elements in the stack after each operation.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of operations where each operation is a string representing 'push val', 'pop', 'max', or 'min'. The value val will be a non-negative integer.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the count of elements in the stack after each operation.
statement.notes: You should handle stack overflow and underflow correctly.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> solve(vector<vector<string>>& ops); };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public: int[] solve(String[][] ops); }
starterCode.python:
class Solution: def solve(self, ops: List[List[str]]) -> List[int]:
    pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> solve(vector<vector<string>>& ops) { vector<int> ans; for (auto& op : ops) { if (op[0] == "push") ans.push_back(stoi(op[1])); else if (op[0] == "pop" && !ans.empty()) ans.pop_back(); else if (op[0] == "max") { if (!ans.empty()) cout << *max_element(ans.begin(), ans.end()) << endl; else cout << -1 << endl; } else if (op[0] == "min") { if (!ans.empty()) cout << *min_element(ans.begin(), ans.end()) << endl; else cout << -1 << endl; } } return ans; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public: int[] solve(String[][] ops) { int[] ans = new int[ops.length]; for (int i = 0; i < ops.length; i++) { if (ops[i][0].equals("push")) ans[i] = Integer.parseInt(ops[i][1]); else if (ops[i][0].equals("pop") && ans[i] != 0) ans[i] = 0; else if (ops[i][0].equals("max")) { if (ans[i] != 0) System.out.println(Integer.MAX_VALUE); else System.out.println(-1); } else if (ops[i][0].equals("min")) { if (ans[i] != 0) System.out.println(Integer.MIN_VALUE); else System.out.println(-1); } } return ans; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, ops: List[List[str]]) -> List[int]: ans = []; for op in ops: if op[0] == "push": ans.append(int(op[1])); else if op[0] == "pop" and ans: ans.pop(); else if op[0] == "max": if ans: print(max(ans)); else print(-1); else if op[0] == "min": if ans: print(min(ans)); else print(-1); return ans;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(ops) { let ans = []; for (let op of ops) { if (op[0] == "push") ans.push(parseInt(op[1])); else if (op[0] == "pop" && ans.length) ans.pop(); else if (op[0] == "max") { if (ans.length) console.log(Math.max(...ans)); else console.log(-1); } else if (op[0] == "min") { if (ans.length) console.log(Math.min(...ans)); else console.log(-1); } } return ans; }
